commit.message:
Refactor web chat transcript and run state (#363)

* refactor(web): normalize chat transcript and run state

Summary:
- add shared web chat types and a local normalized transcript hook
- store messages by id/order and runs by run_id in the chat page
- derive evidence panel state from run-linked records and sync the
  web lockfile to the checked-in dependencies

Rationale:
- remove client_id and array-scan updates from streamed and
  action-required chat flows
- keep loaded sessions, trace selection, and source resolution tied
  to the latest run record
- keep the web workspace installable so lint and build validation can
  run against the committed dependency graph

Tests:
- cd apps/axiom-web && pnpm lint
- cd apps/axiom-web && pnpm exec tsc --noEmit
- cd apps/axiom-web && pnpm build
- manual smoke via output/playwright-smoke/manual-smoke.js against a
  local mock API

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(ci): restore repo lint and API test setup

Summary:
- fix repo-wide ruff violations in the legacy GUI module and API tests
- add the missing Weaviate lazy-import helper used by the legacy GUI
- install the API extra in CI and include python-multipart in the
  project API dependencies

Rationale:
- the PR test matrix was failing before pytest because repo-level lint
  issues in untouched Python files were red
- the Linux test job installs only `.[dev]`, so API tests would fail on
  FastAPI upload route imports once lint passed
- keeping these fixes separate from the web refactor makes the branch
  history reviewable while still unblocking green checks

Tests:
- .venv-ci/bin/ruff check . --exclude .venv-ci
- .venv-ci/bin/python -m pytest -q
- .venv-ci/bin/python main.py --cli --help

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* test(ci): skip action card test without Qt runtime

Summary:
- gate the action card widget test behind importorskip for the Qt
  modules and dependent view modules
- align the skip behavior with the rest of the Qt-focused test suite

Rationale:
- the Linux test matrix was failing during collection when PySide6 was
  importable but its runtime EGL libraries were unavailable
- the widget test should skip in that environment instead of crashing
  before the rest of the suite can run

Tests:
- .venv-ci/bin/ruff check . --exclude .venv-ci
- .venv-ci/bin/python -m pytest -q
- .venv-ci/bin/python main.py --cli --help

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(codex): harden PR lookup and auto review

Summary:
- fix runtime ticket delivery to resolve PR URLs with a valid
  branch-scoped gh pr view invocation
- extend app-server coverage for both PR create and existing-PR edit
  delivery paths
- add an explicit Claude review prompt to the workflow_run review job
  and guard it in repository tests

Rationale:
- GitHub CLI rejects gh pr view with --repo alone, which blocked
  runtime delivery after PR creation
- the external review workflow should run automatically after CI
  instead of depending on @claude mentions
- repository guard tests should lock both behaviors in to prevent
  regressions

Tests:
- ./scripts/verify

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

test(websocket): add recker integration coverage

Summary:
- add recker as a dev dependency for websocket integration testing
- cover a Raffel custom websocket server with a Recker client
- verify a welcome frame plus 10 request and response exchanges

Rationale:
- this proves Raffel interoperates with Recker over a real websocket session
- it also gives us a regression test for multi-message custom protocol flows

Tests:
- pnpm vitest run -c vitest.config.int.ts test/adapters/websocket-custom-protocol.int.test.ts
- pnpm typecheck

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

chore(repo): harden delivery and review automation

Summary:
- move runtime ticket delivery onto verify-gated PR publishing and
  template-faithful PR content
- extract a pure status request handler and rewrite status server tests
  to avoid binding localhost sockets in sandboxes
- update the GitHub review workflow for the current AWS region and
  Claude model routing

Rationale:
- successful ticket completion must stop before commit, push, PR, and
  Linear comment when repo verification fails
- status observability tests need to pass in restricted environments
  where loopback listeners are blocked
- the review workflow needs current cloud and model configuration to
  stay non-interactive and reliable

Tests:
- ./scripts/verify

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(websocket): reject invalid connections during handshake

Summary:
- move websocket filter and auth checks into handshake validation
- preserve auth seed from the upgrade request for accepted connections
- update integration tests to assert 401 and 403 handshake failures

Rationale:
- invalid websocket requests should fail before the upgrade completes
- this avoids transient opens followed by close events in browser clients

Tests:
- pnpm vitest run -c vitest.config.int.ts test/adapters/websocket.int.test.ts
- pnpm typecheck

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

compact parallel frame buffer in place

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(codex): own ticket delivery at runtime

Summary:
- add a complete_ticket_delivery tool that commits, pushes, creates or
  updates the PR, and posts the final Linear completion comment
- pass issue delivery metadata through AgentRunner and use structured
  delivery results as the primary success signal
- update the workflow prompt and repository guards to require the
  runtime-owned completion path

Rationale:
- prompt-only PR and comment instructions let Codex finish work without
  actually publishing the branch or including a PR URL in the handoff
- moving the delivery sequence into runtime code makes completion
  deterministic and lets the runner fail before posting an invalid
  completion comment

Tests:
- ./scripts/verify
- npm run build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
test(saas_client): add transport/retry/unhappy-path coverage (issue #34) (#35)

Adds 11 new scenarios covering branches not exercised by prior tests:
- Transport error paths for event flush, heartbeat, config pull, config ack
- Register non-retriable (401) and retriable (503) HTTP error statuses
- Event flush backoff suppression: second flush blocked until window expires
- Flush returns 0 when circuit breaker is open (before half-open window)
- Config pull non-retriable 403 does not apply bundle
- Config ack transport error does not prevent bundle from being applied
- queue_event with non-table argument returns validation error
- Heartbeat triggers periodic config pull when poll interval has elapsed

534 unit + 65 integration tests passing, luacheck clean.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Summary:

Rationale:

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

chore(workflow): harden PR publish guidance

Summary:
- rewrite workspace origins after local clone so workspace git pushes target
  the source repository's GitHub remote
- keep WORKFLOW.md focused on ticket delivery policy and move the exact
  non-interactive PR publish procedure into the push skill
- extend repository guard tests and operations notes to lock in the push
  workflow and workspace remote behavior

Rationale:
- agent workspaces need a usable GitHub origin to push branches and open
  pull requests reliably
- de-duplicating publish instructions reduces drift between the workflow
  prompt and the reusable push skill

Tests:
- npx vitest run test/repository/tooling.test.ts
  test/repository/workflow.test.ts
- npx prettier --check WORKFLOW.md .codex/skills/push/SKILL.md
  docs/operations.md test/repository/tooling.test.ts
  test/repository/workflow.test.ts

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(web): clear chat lint and build blockers (#359)

Summary:
- replace synchronous effect-driven resets in the evidence panel
  and index picker with guarded prop sync plus async fetch updates
- switch the evidence source tooltip trigger to the Base UI
  render prop API
- widen the chat panel composer ref prop type to match the page
  usage that surfaced once the original build blocker was removed

Rationale:
- clears the current chat frontend lint violations without changing
  the user flows those effects were coordinating
- preserves the copy-button tooltip behavior using the tooltip API
  this repo actually exposes
- keeps the fix narrowly scoped to the files needed for lint/build
  to pass on this branch

Tests:
- pnpm lint
- pnpm build

commit.message:
fix(ci): pin npm registry to public npmjs

Summary:
- add a repo-level npm registry config for the public npmjs registry
- regenerate the lockfile so resolved package URLs no longer point at a
  private Nexus host
- add a repository guard that prevents private registry settings from leaking
  back into tracked npm inputs

Rationale:
- make GitHub Actions installs deterministic and independent of local user npm
  configuration
- prevent future CI failures caused by lockfiles generated against private
  workstation registries

Tests:
- npx vitest run test/repository/tooling.test.ts
- ./scripts/verify

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(review): sync blocking GitHub review feedback to Linear

Summary:
- add a github-review workflow that runs after successful make-all PR runs
- mirror blocking GitHub review findings into Linear and move issues back to
  Rework through a dedicated sync script
- require Linear issue identifiers in PR bodies and include recent Linear
  comments in the agent prompt for rework runs

Rationale:
- make external GitHub review part of the normal PR flow without giving GitHub
  authority over work state
- keep Linear as the single source of truth for rework dispatch while still
  surfacing reviewer feedback back to Symphony
- enforce a stable PR-to-Linear mapping so review results can be synced
  deterministically

Tests:
- ./scripts/verify

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
chore(repo): align workflow and ci conventions

Summary:
- pin CI and local defaults to Node 24 LTS and update GitHub Actions
- require branch and PR handoff details in the shipped workflow prompt
- remove stale Elixir-specific repo tooling references and cache cleanup

Rationale:
- stabilize GitHub Actions by avoiding the flaky Node 25 path and old action
  runtimes
- make the agent handoff contract match the intended branch and PR flow
- keep repo docs, skills, and workspace cleanup aligned with the current
  TypeScript implementation

Tests:
- ./scripts/verify

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Add a multilingual profile README for the reseller-guru
organization with a concise description of what the team
builds and the people it hopes to meet.

AI-authored: 94%
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

test(coverage): targeted unit tests for decision_api and saas_client (Issue #30) (#31)

Add 29 new scenarios across decision_api and saas_client to raise coverage:

decision_api.lua:
- Jitter hash fallbacks (crc32_short, hmac_sha256, sha1_bin unavailable)
- Provider detection (anthropic, openai_compatible, gemini, mistral, nil)
- ip_tor normalization via normalize_boolish (yes/no/1/0)
- Bundle descriptor hint suppresses user_agent when needs_user_agent=false
- access_handler 503 paths (rule engine missing, no bundle loaded)
- LLM rejection JSON body for tpm_exceeded and tpd_exceeded
- Retry-after gt_3600 bucket metric
- log_handler upstream error queues saas event, skips 429
- debug_session_handler: 404 (no secret), 405 (GET), 403 (wrong secret), 204 (valid)
- debug_logout_handler: 404 (no secret), 405 (GET), 204 (POST)

saas_client.lua:
- flush_events/pull_config guard when not initialized
- Edge token CR/LF injection uses empty bearer auth
- Event coalescing: same signature increments counter, summary on flush
- Subject ID hashing removes raw field, sets subject_id_hash
- Non-retriable 401 on event flush counts as error
- Bundle load rejection ack as rejected
- Half-open circuit failure reopens circuit
- Heartbeat JSON string body extract_payload branch
- Timer premature=true callback returns without HTTP request

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
test(coverage): targeted unit tests for Issue #25 coverage gate (#28)

* test(coverage): add targeted unit tests for issue #25 coverage gate

Add new test cases for previously uncovered branches in utils.lua,
streaming.lua, and bundle_loader.lua to push non-generated coverage
above the 82.28% baseline:

- utils: now() no-ngx, safe_require, to_hex, constant_time_equals,
  encode_base64 no-ngx, decode_base64 fallback, base64url_decode edge
  cases, sha256 nil guard, json additional branches, get_json chain
- streaming: validate_config type errors, is_streaming nil/non-table,
  body_filter truncated/buffer paths, include_partial_usage=false
- bundle_loader: ua descriptor hint, sha1-fallback HMAC, hmac
  unavailable, md5/pure hash fallbacks, no-timer init, file_not_found,
  saas_client audit event on apply

Closes #25

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(lua-quality): remove duplicate local utils declaration in utils_spec

luacheck warned: variable utils re-defined on line 260, already declared
on line 3. Remove the redundant require at the Issue #25 section header.

commit.message:
feat: add visual deploy verification (#38)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

docs(agents): document stale pr-opened handling for multi-orch (#425)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(issue-4): use LLM-aware estimator for circuit breaker cost resolution (with E2E) (#23)

* fix(issue-4): use LLM-aware estimator for circuit breaker cost resolution

* test(e2e): add regression test for LLM cost resolution

* test(issue-4): add UC-09/UC-16/UC-17 integration coverage and fix E2E test

- Remove fake test_header_hint.py (had only pass assertions and wrong estimator)
- Add honest header_hint integration test at rule_engine level (circuit breaker
  gets correct cost when X-Token-Estimate header is present)
- UC-09: verify edge evaluates policy correctly when saas_client is unreachable
- UC-16: verify rule_engine produces OpenAI-compatible RateLimit/Retry-After
  headers on tpm_exceeded; rename steps to "decision headers" to clarify these
  are engine-level headers, not HTTP wire contract (decision_api strips
  X-Fairvisor-Reason in non-debug mode — documented in scenario comment)
- UC-17/UC-18: add TK-006 (error_chunk truncation), TK-007 (shadow mode
  passthrough), TK-008 (truncation without partial usage) to streaming spec
- Add limit/remaining fields to llm_limiter reject result so _apply_limit_headers
  can populate RateLimit-Limit and RateLimit-Remaining headers

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(test): make UC-09 actually exercise saas_client failure path

Previously the UC-09 scenario used an allow decision, which exits before
calling queue_event — so saas_unreachable flag had no effect on the test.

Now uses a reject decision (TPM exceeded) which does call queue_event.
Track saas_event_attempts in mock to assert the call was made.
New assertion: queue_event was attempted AND the decision is still valid,
proving SaaS audit failure does not block enforcement.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(e2e): P0 e2e tests for Issue #4 — OpenAI contract and streaming truncation

- decision_api.lua: wire llm_limiter.build_error_response in reject path;
  tpm_exceeded/tpd_exceeded/prompt_tokens_exceeded/max_tokens_per_request_exceeded
  now return Content-Type: application/json with OpenAI error structure before ngx.exit(429)
- tests/e2e/test_llm_openai_contract.py: 5 tests verifying 429 body is valid OpenAI
  JSON (error.type=rate_limit_error, error.code=rate_limit_exceeded, message references reason)
  plus Content-Type and RateLimit-* headers still present
- tests/e2e/test_llm_streaming.py: 5 tests verifying mid-stream truncation at
  max_completion_tokens=10 — events 1+2 pass, event 3 triggers truncation,
  response ends with finish_reason=length and [DONE]
- tests/e2e/policy_llm_openai_contract.json: TPM=500, header_hint estimator
- tests/e2e/policy_llm_streaming.json: TPM=100k, defaults.max_completion_tokens=10,
  streaming.buffer_tokens=1, graceful_close mode
- tests/e2e/sse_fixture.txt: 3 SSE events (4+5+6 tokens) + [DONE]
- tests/e2e/mock-streaming-backend.conf: nginx serving sse_fixture.txt as SSE
- docker-compose.test.yml: edge_llm_openai_contract (18089), mock_streaming_backend,
  edge_llm_streaming (18090)
- conftest.py: fixtures for edge_llm_openai_contract_base_url and edge_llm_streaming_base_url

All 497 unit+integration tests continue to pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ci): resolve code-quality warnings and add new e2e tests to smoke CI

- test_llm_openai_contract.py: remove unused 'import json'
- test_llm_streaming.py: remove unused 'import pytest'
- conftest.py: invert fixture conditionals (if not ready: skip / return url)
  to eliminate mixed implicit/explicit return warnings from static analysis
- bin/ci/run_e2e_smoke.sh: add test_llm_openai_contract.py and
  test_llm_streaming.py to smoke test suite so new E2E tests are
  gated in CI

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(e2e): replace nginx streaming mock with Python server to fix 405 on POST

nginx's static file module only handles GET/HEAD — serving sse_fixture.txt
via try_files returned 405 for POST /v1/chat/completions, causing all 5
streaming e2e tests to fail.

Replace mock_streaming_backend with a minimal Python http.server that:
- Accepts POST on any path → returns SSE fixture with correct Content-Type
- Accepts GET / → returns "ok" (used by healthcheck)
- Embeds the SSE content inline (4+5+6 token events for truncation test)

Remove broken mock-streaming-backend.conf and sse_fixture.txt.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(streaming): preserve buffered events when truncation fires mid-batch

When nginx buffers multiple SSE events into a single body_filter call,
the early return on truncation was discarding events that had already
passed the token budget check and been added to `out`.

Fix: return table_concat(out) .. _build_termination(ctx) so that events
processed before the truncation threshold are forwarded to the client.

For single-event calls (the common streaming case), out is empty so
table_concat({}) = "" — identical behavior to before.

This was caught by the e2e streaming truncation test where the Python
mock sends the complete SSE fixture as a single HTTP response body.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
